principle: One hero and one plot per sentence — if the sentence doesn't know what it's about, split it
explanation: X.copy|one_hero: sentence_must_have_one_subject; cramming_multiple_actors+events -> unreadable; ask "who_is_the_hero?"; split_rest_into_next_sentences; includes_compound_clauses_with_no_link; NO:multi-plot_sentences eg: "man_slipped_breaking_car_window_with_bottle_nearly_hitting_child" >> "man_slipped._Bottle_hit_car._Glass_flew_toward_child"

principle: Cursor-back trap: author inserts details backwards — reader gets full complexity at once. Introduce gradually, keep chronology
explanation: X.copy|cursor_back: author_writes_simple_sentence -> inserts_details_backwards_5x; author_has_context_each_time; reader_sees_ALL_at_once == overload; also_breaks_chronology; NO:nesting_circumstances_into_existing_text; introduce_one_detail_per_sentence; maintain_time_order eg: "25jan_such_Ermolaev_at_ceremony_of..." >> "children_centers_launch._Announced_by_X_and_Y._Ceremony_held_at..."

principle: Near-synonym homogeneous members: reader sees no difference — keep one or replace with a fact
explanation: X.copy|near_synonyms: "quickly_precisely_punctually" -> reader_cant_distinguish; keep_one OR replace_all_with_fact; 3+_distinct_items -> list_with_intro_phrase; NO:padding_evaluations_with_synonyms eg: "deliver_fast_precise_punctual" >> "deliver_in_30_minutes"

principle: Overdried text: after cutting fluff the remaining facts choke the reader — split dense sentences and space out information
explanation: X.copy|overdried: watery_text -> cut_fluff -> cram_all_facts_into_one_sentence -> reader_chokes; water_was_invisible_padding; now_every_word_demands_attention; NO:replacing_water_with_brick; split_facts_across_sentences eg: "since_2003_dispatching_warehouse_tracking_shipping_ISO" >> 3_sentences: what_we_do | certification | year_founded

principle: Flatten complex grammar: nested subordination, paired conjunctions, indirect speech — reader holds too much in memory
explanation: X.copy|flatten_grammar: "because...when...which" x2_levels -> reader_loses_thread; "not_only...but_also" -> holds_part_1_while_reading_part_2; "he_said_that..." -> often_"that"_clause_IS_the_sentence; digressions: worth_own_paragraph? extract; not? delete; NO:hierarchical_constructions eg: "not_only_list_but_also_estimate" >> "three_tasks:_list,_sort,_estimate"
